title: Peucedanum typicum
intro: Peucedanum typicum, also known as Chinese angelica tree or typical peucedanum, is a perennial plant species belonging to the carrot family (Apiaceae). This plant has been traditionally used in medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may aid in reducing stress and promoting overall well-being. The bioactive constituents of Peucedanum typicum include volatile oils, flavonoids, alkaloids, and coumarins, which are thought to contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, Peucedanum typicum is commonly used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues.

This family is a large and diverse group of plants that includes many species of herbs, shrubs, and trees. The genus Peucedanum is a group of plants within the Apiaceae family, characterized by their umbel-like inflorescences and often aromatic foliage. Further classification of Peucedanum typicum would typically include its binomial nomenclature and possibly its cultivar or subspecies designations.
